findings.summary: Unit 1-D’s storage sketch still misuses Maestro’s remote-fs helper APIs.
findings.evidence: The plan calls `readDirRemote(sessionDir, sshConfig)` at plan.md:570, `readFileRemote(filePath, sshConfig, { maxBytes: 8192 })` at plan.md:578, `statRemote(filePath, sshConfig)` at plan.md:584, and `readFileRemote(filePath, sshConfig)` at plan.md:622 even though `sshConfig` is optional in the enclosing methods. Live Maestro defines `readDirRemote`, `readFileRemote`, and `statRemote` with required `SshRemoteConfig` parameters and no `{ maxBytes }` overload (`src/main/utils/remote-fs.ts:209-345`). The reference `CodexSessionStorage` branches on `if (sshConfig)` before using remote helpers and falls back to local `fs` APIs otherwise (`src/main/storage/codex-session-storage.ts:803-805,942-958,1121-1130`).
findings.impact: An implementer following the plan literally will still produce a storage sketch that is not contract-correct for local vs SSH execution and may not type-check because the sample invokes `readFileRemote(...)` with an unsupported third argument.
findings.recommended_fix: Rewrite the Unit 1-D sketch to mirror the Codex storage pattern: branch on `if (sshConfig)` before calling remote helpers, use local `fs` APIs when `sshConfig` is absent, and remove the unsupported `{ maxBytes: 8192 }` argument from `readFileRemote(...)`.
